subject: Math
requirement: Understand vertex form, standard form, direction of opening, axis of symmetry, and transformations of quadratic graphs.
explanation: Quadratic functions can be written as y = ax² + bx + c or y = a(x-h)² + k. The sign of a determines whether the parabola opens up or down, and (h, k) is the vertex in vertex form.
remote_source: CK-12 / Khan Academy / Open Trivia DB

subject: Physics
requirement: Distinguish speed, velocity and acceleration, and solve basic constant-acceleration problems.
explanation: Velocity includes direction. Acceleration measures the rate of change in velocity. A common formula is a = (vf - vi) / t.
remote_source: CK-12 / Khan Academy / Open Trivia DB

subject: Chemistry
requirement: Identify protons, neutrons, electrons, atomic number, and mass number.
explanation: Atoms contain protons and neutrons in the nucleus and electrons around the nucleus. Atomic number equals proton count.
remote_source: CK-12 / Khan Academy / Open Trivia DB

subject: Math
requirement: Model slope, intercepts, and graphs of linear relationships.
explanation: A linear function has constant rate of change. In y = mx + b, m is the slope and b is the y-intercept.
remote_source: CK-12 / Khan Academy / Open Trivia DB

subject: Math
requirement: Understand basic function notation, domain, range, and graph interpretation.
explanation: A function maps each input to exactly one output. Domain is the set of valid inputs and range is the set of outputs.
